candidates.stmt:
Martin Price is the Green Party ward candidate for Ashbrow on Kirkees Council.
He lives in Kirklees and is an active member of Mirfield in Bloom, a 'Recycling Champion Hero' and a trustee of a Church of England owned ancient woodland in Birstall. Martin has worked in UK and international business management for many years, attaining director positions in two companies. His specialism is pharmaceutical and medical device manufacturing, with a focus on product compliance and IT systems. He and his family have lived in the Kirklees for over 20 years. Early in his career Martin spent 6 years working in Zimbabwe, Africa. Being a practical person he was challenged on many levels but successfully completed significant projects included sanitation, clean water and school rebuilding. Martin says:  "I would prioritise measures that encourage and safeguard biodiversity in our wonderful green spaces and aim to improve access. Protection of the natural environment is an essential part of tackling the warming of our planet. "If elected as your councillor I would work tirelessly to improve Ashbrow for the benefit of all members of our wonderful diverse community. We need to create a fairer, greener country that is fit for future generations. It's time to put Ashbrow in a safe pair of hands - I would be accountable to the people who elected me."
Promoted by Andrew Cooper on behalf of Kirklees Green Party at 76 Brockholes Lane, Holmfirth HD9 7EB

Andy Parr has a masters in Business Adminstration and is a qualified Anti-Money Laundering specialist. He served with the Royal Navy as a Marine Engineer travelling the globe. He has visited both Polar Regions, the West Indies and all over Europe and the Baltic region.
In 1992 he joined West Yorkshire Police. As a constable he was a self-defence instructor and a firearms officer. On promotion to Sergeant he served at Deighton in Huddersfield leading a response and community team. He then led the Street Crime Initiative and moved into CID. He was promoted to Detective Inspector and has managed several serious and organised crime investigations including homicide. 
He improved the Kirklees police detection rate to 26% and reduced gun crime in the district by 56%. He led a change programme in the district saving £15 million by re-organising policing response, crime allocation and investigation.

In 2015 he deployed to Afghanistan to mentor an Afghan Colonel in criminal intelligence.

In 2018 in moved to the Cayman Islands working in Anti-Money laundering, protecting the international financial system from criminal abuse.

Andy is running for the Ashbrow ward in Kirklees on a ticket of improving policing services and community safety.

candidates.p: People's Alliance for Change and Equality

candidates.stmt:
I’m Akhtar Kasia, a local resident, business owner, and someone who genuinely cares about Batley and the people who live here.

I’m standing as an independent because I don’t believe our town should be run by party politics. Decisions should be based on what’s right for Batley, not what suits a party agenda.

Over the years, I’ve been actively involved in the community, speaking to residents, supporting local initiatives, and highlighting the real issues people face day to day. The same concerns keep coming up: cleanliness, accountability, lack of investment, and slow or no action from those in charge.

We all pay into the system. We all deserve better standards.

If elected, I will focus on raising standards across the board. That means holding the council to account, pushing for visible improvements in our streets and services, and making sure your voice is heard where it matters.

I won’t disappear after the election. I’m already out there, and that won’t change.

This isn’t about promises. It’s about doing the job properly.

If you want someone who will speak up, challenge where needed, and represent Batley with honesty and energy, I’d appreciate your support.

My name is Fouz Ul Azeem Butt and I am standing as your Labour candidate for Batley East in the local elections on Thursday 7 May.
I am a local business owner in Kirklees, with over a decade of experience creating jobs and working closely with customers and communities. Through my work in retail, I see first-hand the pressures families are facing every day, from rising costs to overstretched local services.
I have been an active Labour Party member since 2015 and have campaigned across Kirklees for over a decade. For me, it has always been about listening to residents and working hard to get things done.
If elected, my priorities for Batley East will be:
	•	Fixing roads and tackling the pothole backlog
	•	Cracking down on fly-tipping and keeping our streets clean
	•	Supporting and revitalising Batley Town Centre
	•	Protecting and repairing Batley Library
	•	Addressing anti-social behaviour and improving community safety
	•	Tackling nuisance parking and improving access for residents and businesses
	•	Securing long-term burial provision for all communities in Batley
	•	Supporting families through the cost of living crisis
	•	Creating better opportunities for young people
I believe Batley East deserves a representative who is visible, accountable and committed all year round, not just at election time.
I am here to listen as much as I speak, so please feel free to get in touch with your concerns and ideas.

Local. Independent. On your side. Born in Dewsbury Hospital, raised in Staincliffe, at the heart of our community.

Statement: 
Independent. Local. On Your Side.

Dear Resident,
Batley West isn’t just where we live, it’s our community, and it deserves to come first.

I’m asking for your support to continue serving you as your local councillor focused on people, not party politics.

Through my work in the NHS as a mental health professional, and as a long-standing community campaigner, I understand the pressures local families face because I see them every day and live them too.

Over the past eight years, I’ve worked to deliver real results for Batley West supporting residents with local issues, standing up for transparency in council decisions, and helping local businesses through challenging times. I believe being a councillor means being visible, approachable, and getting things done.

In 2024, I made the difficult decision to step away from party politics because I could no longer support decisions that were not putting our community first.

I answer to you, not a political party.

Across Batley West, I hear the same concerns: rising costs, stretched services, and people feeling unheard. You deserve a councillor who listens and takes action.

I will continue working with you to:
Keep our streets safe and tackle local concerns
Maintain cleaner, well-cared-for neighbourhoods
Support both physical and mental health in our community
Invest in young people and local community projects
Ensure fair, open, and transparent decision-making

Being your councillor means being present, listening, and acting, working side by side with residents to build a stronger, safer Batley West.

This election isn’t about party labels, it’s about who will stand up for you.

If you want a councillor who listens, is visible, and delivers for Batley West, I ask for your vote.

Together, we can keep Batley West moving forward.

At your service,
Cllr Yusra Hussain

I’m proud to be standing as your Conservative candidate for Batley West in the local elections on Thursday 7th May.
I’m not a career politician—I’m a local resident. I live here, work here, and care deeply about our community. I’ve spent years supporting local groups, schools, and young people because I believe in creating better opportunities for everyone.
Like you, I see the same issues every day—fly-tipping, unsafe parking, and potholes that never seem to get fixed. These problems affect our safety, our daily lives, and our pride in Batley West.
It’s time for change.
My priorities are simple:
• Cleaner streets
• Safer neighbourhoods
• Properly repaired roads
• Support for local businesses
• Tackling dangerous parking
• Better community spaces
This isn’t about politics—it’s about people and making Batley West better for everyone.
On Thursday 7th May, you have a choice: more of the same, or real change.
I’m ready to listen, work hard, and deliver for you.
I’d be grateful for your support.
Let’s put Batley West first

Philip Anthony James, aged 51, was born in Thorpe Maternity Hospital, Sunderland on 14th September 1968. In 1974 his family moved to Brighouse, West Yorkshire, where he has lived ever since. 

Philip attended All Saints’ High School in Huddersfield and subsequently The Percival Whitley School of Further Education in Halifax. Philip has worked in the construction industry at both grass roots and executive level. He is currently a registered gas engineer and owns a small business in West Yorkshire. 

Philip is married with children and lives in Hove Edge, Brighouse. He is a valued member of the local community and a sponsor for the local cricket club in Southowram. He is a keen sportsman and anxious to promote the importance of sport for young people in the midst of the ‘techno age’ 

As a parent, Philip is also interested in education and the importance of language learning. He has volunteered as an English conversation teacher, at a school in Madrid, on many occasions, in collaboration with a bilingual learning programme set up by one of his Spanish friends. 

Although Philip is a newcomer to the political scene, he has previously negotiated and completed contracts with some of the UK’s listed PLC construction companies. This wide experience in the business world along with a passionate interest in history and world affairs have led him to enter the political scene.



Dewsbury is a 57.15% Leave area and is very winnable.

Tahir has lived in the constituency for over ten years in Birkenshaw where he is married with two daughters; Jasmine aged 6 and Layla 2. He works in football development and also volunteers his time coaching the girls under 7s side at Birkenshaw Primary School. 

 Speaking after his selection as a candidate, Tahir said: "I'm passionate about climate change and social equality, which is why I am standing for the Greens; the only political party genuinely committed to tacking the climate emergency and bringing about the social justice this country so desperately needs."

John Lawson lives in the heart of Cleckheaton with his family. He is the only candidate for the Cleckheaton ward to live in the area of the ward.
John was first elected in 2010 and is a key member of the Liberal Democrat team in Cleckheaton who work all year round for local people and the town.
John and the Lib Dem team keep residents informed through a regular FOCUS newsletter; ask residents for their views and take action on their behalf. They hold councillor advice and help surgeries EVERY Saturday in Cleckheaton and Scholes and once a month in Oakenshaw.
John is involved in supporting many of the local community groups for example helping the Friends of Scholes Rec develop the once neglected Council-owned open space in Scholes.
John is campaigning in this election against plans agreed by Labour-run Kirklees Council to impose parking charges on the town's free car parks. A petition in support of the campaign has attracted nearly 7,000 signatures and is likely to be successful in persuading the Council to drop the plans.
John and the team are fighting hard as they have done for the last 3 or more years to save the Library in Cleckheaton from closure. It is the only safe place that is freely open to all. It is in an iconic building that was donated to the town. It serves around 50,000 people in the Spen Valley and MUST be kept open.
Potholes - despite the best efforts of Highways services our roads are in a very poor state. the Lib Dems proposed an additional £1m each year to repair potholes but this was turned down by Labour councillors.
£45m allocated for investment in Dewsbury and Huddersfield while taking services away from Cleckheaton is unacceptable.

New blood replacing the old, stopping the same same tired run of the mill politics I no interest in self-gain or the House of Lords gravy train I want to bring a business mind and common sense to Kirklees, which it lacks 
It’s time to change new views, new ideas, and a new way of thinking 
I'm always available and contactable not just during elections 
I've accomplished so much with out even been a Cllr imaging what I can do when I am 
Contact me any time 07762156250 or piers@techiecoms.co.uk

I'm Michael Simmons, and I'm standing in Colne Valley East

I've lived in Huddersfield for over 20 years in different areas including Longwood and Paddock.

I previously worked for Kirklees Council for 7 years, and have witnessed council waste first hand.

I spent 4 years in China teaching English.

Andrew Marchington lives in the heart of the Golcar ward with his family and knows the area very well having lived there for much of his life.
Andrew has served local people as their councillor for a number of years. Andrew and the Lib Dem team hold regular help and advice surgeries and have helped many hundreds of local residents with individual problems.
Andrew keeps local people informed with a regular FOCUS newsletter; listens to their views and takes action on their behalf.
Andrew Marchington and the team are campaigning about the state of roads and pavement. Kirklees Highways simply does not have sufficient funding to make our roads safe. The Lib Dems proposed £1m extra for Highways in the Council's Budget but this was turned down by Kirklees Labour councillors.
Too much of the green space available in the Golcar ward area has been committed to housing. Of course more houses are needed but this needs to be planned thoughtfully with the needs of existing residents in mind. This means protecting green spaces; providing sufficient facilities such as school places and doctors surgeries. Sadly, this is not happening under Labour.
Labour councillors have consistently refused to even consider finding a solution to the horrendous traffic congestion that local people and businesses have to endure on a daily basis. Milnsbridge is gridlocked at some point every day, queues of traffic in the morning and evening are growing longer - local Labour refuse to do anything.
The villages and communities that make up the ward are neglected by Labour. Sad but true. Andrew is a committed and energetic councillor who always speaks up for local people.

I’m running because I’m passionate about improving and investing our communities (both in Colne Valley East & Kirklees more widely), as well as providing an alternative to the decades of declining services – especially in regards to fuelling anti-social behaviour

As a student at the University of Huddersfield I am eager to learn and want to bring that energy to Town Hall

Young voices like mine are routinely put-down as lacking experience but I believe that we simply have different but still equally valid amounts of experience, making it important that younger voices like mine are also represented on our council

Previously I have worked with my local parish council, through petitioning the local community, to get a bus shelter instated at a frequently used bus stop
According to Companis House there is a Stephan Hugh Dransfield at Green Lane Farm, Slaithwaite, Huddersfield, England, HD7 5XA

Working with the community, we have achieved a lot over the past few years

🚶🦽🚲 Resurfacing the canal towpath between Marsden and Huddersfield
🛝 Playground improvements at Causeway Crescent in Linthwaite and Marsden Dirker Rec
🛑 Stopping cuts to Marsden and Slaithwaite libraries
🍎 Creating a community orchard in Linthwaite
🤝 Supporting local events and community groups
...and much more

This election will be close race between Labour and Reform, who want to use our valley as a springboard for their division and chaos. If re-elected, we pledge to focus on the community's priorities, including

👮 More police patrols to tackle speeding and dangerous driving - working with the police to fund extra highways safety shifts, proactively targeting dangerous driving hotspots
🍞 Setting up a hardship fund - working with local community groups to support those unable to purchase household items
💡 Securing the future of our leisure centre - delivering an energy efficiency project at Colne Valley Leisure Centre to cut down on energy costs, making it more sustainable
🧑‍🧑‍🧒‍🧒 Supporting our young people - tackling antisocial behaviour by funding detached youth workers in Linthwaite helping young people in the Colne Valley at risk of turning to crime, and upgrading the playground at Slaithwaite Spa
🏭 Redeveloping Marsden New Mills - delivering new jobs and car parking in the centre of the village

Please use your three votes for Jayne Fisher, Harry McCarthy and Matthew McLoughlin to stop Reform.

I was born and raised in Thornton Lodge, Huddersfield, and this community has shaped who I am. For me, the Crosland Moor ward is my home and identity.

After completing my studies in 2015, I returned to serve the community, working closely with families and young people. It was here that I saw both the strength of our community and the lack of opportunities for many of our youth. So I took action, helping to establish a youth club that gave dozens of young people a safe space each week.

In 2018, I founded Huddersfield Community First with my own savings. Since then, we’ve supported thousands through youth programmes, education, and vital food provision including a food bank, soup kitchen and hardship grants, because I believe no one in our town should be left behind.

But despite the hard work of local people, our area is being let down. Services are declining, costs are rising, and too many voices are going unheard. Crosland Moor deserves better.

That’s why I’m standing as an independent candidate. Free from party control, and fully accountable to you. I’m not a career politician. I’m someone who has served this community for over a decade and will continue to do so with honesty, action, and purpose.

I pledge to:
1. Challenge proposed cuts and improve local services including, restoring parks, improving roads, and tackling fly-tipping.
2. Ensure ward budgets are fully invested in local initiatives.
3. Work with WYP to reduce crime and prioritise public safety.
4. Champion local projects, particularly youth provisions.
5. Donate at least £200 every month from my personal allowance to local community projects.

Miri Finch Is Revolting

Revolting against state overreach. 
Revolting against corruption.
Revolting against the systematic erosion of civil liberties, human rights, and personal freedoms.

But, perhaps most emphatically of all, revolting against the cost of living crisis - such as paying over a fiver for a pint! (We would pledge to make a range of Revolting beverages available to suit all budgets.)

So, say it loud, say it proud (and we did briefly consider the name, 'Conspiracy Realists And Proud', before realising there were some slight acronym issues...)

WE ARE REVOLTING!

If you are too, join us!

https://revoltingparty.co.uk

We have suffered under 30 years of managed decline. Closed assets. Rising crime. Council tax increased year on year to the maximum amount. The same approach has delivered the same results.
I’m standing to challenge that.

• Push for a Full audit of council spending. Every penny accounted for. If it can’t be justified, then it should be cut.
• No more vanity projects. I will push for services that benefit the people not the institution.
• Fight the two tier investment imbalance. I will lobby relentlessly to ensure that Dewsbury East is not be left behind while other areas are prioritised.
• Push for the full restoration and reopening of Dewsbury Sports Centre. Those who closed it cannot be trusted to save it.
• Push for more police presence and a real plan to tackle all forms of crime, particularly violent crime in the town centre.
• Lobby for investment to restore our once great town centre, and oppose any plan that manages its decline. 
• Protect our green spaces & tackle the issue of fly tipping with a zero tolerance approach. 
• Back local and independent businesses, charities and community groups doing vital work in our ward.
• Independent representation. I am answerable to residents alone, not party politics.
• Oppose every reckless decision, publicly and on record. Residents deserve to see what is happening with their money, where it is being spent & why.

This election is about about common sense, accountability, and putting Dewsbury East first. On May 7th, lend me one of your 3 votes.

I have lived in Dewsbury South all my life.  I care about our area and want to see it flourish.  I want to make sure Dewsbury South gets its fair share of funding to address our needs.  I want to support those people in the ward that have suffered because of the Pandemic.  I want to see the fabric of the ward improved by getting our roads repaired, maintaining our parks, addressing fly tipping and tackling anti-social behaviour.  I want to see investment in facilities for children and young people, so they can play safely and join in organised youth activities.  I want to support additional facilities for the elederly and vulnerable, so they are able to enjoy a better quality of life.

John Rossington has lived in Dewsbury for 15 years He has recently retired as Manager of a service which helped people address their alcohol or drug addiction Previously he ran a service helping people sleeping rough to turn their lives around. John is keen to use his experience to help provide opportunities for those marginalised by society to lead more enriched lives to the benefit of both the individual and the community in which they live.

Simon grew up in rural North Yorkshire before moving to London to attend university. After a spell working in our capital city, he returned to Yorkshire, this time settling in the mill town of Dewsbury. He has made his home here for 20 years, along with his wife Marnie and their two daughters, both of whom attend a local secondary school. As well as his work in the audio visual industry, Simon has also in the past found time to play a pivotal part in the formation of the fan-owned cooperative football club Scarborough Athletic, and served as its first volunteer chair. In addition he served on the board of the national body for Supporters’ Trusts, Supporters Direct, which provides helps provide support and guidance to sports fans seeking to gain ownership and/or board influence of the clubs they follow. In recent years, he has served as a governor of Boothroyd Primary Academy in Dewsbury, gaining first-hand experience of the pressures the education system is under. Having never previously been a dyed-in-the-wool supporter of any particular party, Simon joined the Green Party after researching their policies and realising they actually made a lot of sense. His mission is to get the Green message across to Dewsbury voters!

candidates.stmt:
I'm a Yorkshire family man who is an electrician by trade.

I've seen first hand how the current cost of living crisis is affecting hardworking households. That's why I'm passionate about the Liberal Democrat campaign to make school uniforms more affordable. Please sign our petition using the link below:

https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/641227

Now more than ever it is important that local services are protected and that the Labour administration in Huddersfield is held to account.

wards.results_url: https://www.kirklees.gov.uk/beta/voting-and-elections/pdf/holme-valley-south-results-2026.pdf (spoilt ballots exclude ballots rejected in part)

candidates.stmt:
Living in Yorkshire all my life, settling in New Mill for its community spirit. As a mother 
and grandmother, I am no career politician. I believe we need common sense to bring 
change before our community is destroyed by politicians who simply don't listen.
I truly understand the financial squeeze on households today. Good housekeeping is 
essential; our money must be spent wisely, not on unrealistic vanity projects. I will 
prioritise efficiency to ensure every penny works harder for our families. It is time for a 
representative who understands that money is hard-earned. “I am that person”

POLITICAL IDEOLOGY: Right of Centre, limited government interference, growing economy, low taxation, small state mentality, smarter immigration, support businesses to grow, better future for our children, secure UK.

LOCAL TIES: Lives in Holmfirth with his family, educated in Huddersfield, lived in surrounding area for over 26 years.

WORK AND EDUCATION: Stuart was educated at University of Huddersfield where he achieved a BA with honours in Business Administration, and a further degree in Law. Stuart has worked in retail and manufacturing for over 40 years, in varying management and director roles, with experience of working in both domestic and international markets. He has travelled extensively and understands the importance of the UK on the international stage. He prides himself on turning failing businesses and supporting individuals to achieve their best.

WHY REFORM UK: Join me in making a real difference to British politics. I am passionate about our community and our country. I will always put Britain first, and I am asking you to help me make that change.

We know we can start to rebuild our country, refusing to accept decline and decay. Vote for the ReformUK party on the 4th and we can make a real difference. 

Give me that chance to help turn the corner for Britain. We need change, ReformUK can deliver that change, and I am ready to deliver for my community.

Born in Lindley at Huddersfield Royal Infimary. Brought up in Oakes and lived Locally in Marsh for over 30 years. Went to Lindley Infants School, Lindley Junior School and Salendine Nook High School. Played for YMCA for 10 Years, played for Marsh Liberal Football Team for years, coached Lindley Collegians and Former Chairman of Friends of Greenhead Park.

I believe the local elections are about electing a Councillor who is local, visible and is prepared to help all Lindley Ward residents with their local issues.

My name is Sarah Wood and I am the parliamentary candidate Reform UK Party in the soon-to-be amended seat of Spen Valley (currently Batley & Spen).

I am a professional chartered engineer of 21 years born in Halifax, live within Spen Valley, having spent a lifetime here, connected through the family. I want to be your MP and represent your voice in Parliament.

I am compelled to stand for my home seat of Spen Valley because, as a mother, a wife, and a citizen of this country, I am deeply concerned with the direction those in power are taking this nation I love. This is being felt by me at both a National and Local Level and now pervades every area of my life.

I went from being apathetic about politics to deeply caring about the future of my son’s life in our once amazing Country.

I could not, with all integrity, sit back and watch the shocking disassembly of democratic systems, which I once took for granted, accompanied by the unashamed mass removal of our freedoms.

Everything Now Feels Like One Constant Battle: A fight to get a GP appointment A struggle to get a flight on holiday because of the EU Net Zero Caps A challenge to speak to anyone human to access basic services within 2 hours A confrontation at schools to ensure the safeguarding of my son during his education A grapple with people in positions of medical power to ensure sovereignty over my own body A scrap concerning freedom of movement in my own neighbourhood, let alone in the EU A wrestle with my bank about when and how I may withdraw my money A fracas with the public sector referees who are supposed to be upholding my rights against the behemoth of big business; or a skirmish with employers who seek to steamroller my liberties; And a battle royale for things that we’ve fought and won already, which should have been nailed in their coffins long ago, like Brexit and the deliberate state of our porous borders. In fact, as I say these words, it feels less like a battle and more like – controlled demoli

I am proud to be a Green Party candidate for Liversedge and Gomersal. I moved to Liversedge at age 19 and have developed a real love for the area, but I find myself wishing that we could do more to make the ward the best it can be. 
From flytipping covering our streets, to antisocial behaviour and our limited green spaces disappearing, I would love to work for residents to make Liversedge and Gomersal a home you can be proud of.
The cost of living crisis has made everyday life more difficult for all of us and some have used this to push further division between us all. I believe in hope, not hate. Working together to understand the core issues we face as a ward and pushing for solutions within tight council budgets. As a resident myself, I see many of our problems first-hand, but speaking to residents and understanding the problems that local people are experiencing is the only way to get to the root of our issues.

I am passionate about advocating for our ward issues and promise to push for these. Particular issues I am focused on are: making residents feel safe in our ward, reducing street harassment and abusive behaviour; providing more accessible options of waste disposal to reduce littering and flytipping; pushing for green spaces to be kept green and preserved; holding the council to account on upkeep of council owned land; increasing public transport links to reduce our reliance on cars and parking overcrowding.

I love utilising our greenway and ringway, which give some much-needed doses of nature to our urban ward. I am a beginner cyclist and would not have had the confidence to get out there on my bike without these brilliant off-road spaces. I would love to work alongside councillors from neighbouring wards to improve access to these routes.

I would be honoured to advocate for the residents of Liversedge and Gomersal as your local councillor.

I’m Dan and I am standing as a candidate in Liversedge and Gomersal as I believe we can make a real difference in our ward. I want to support local people to work alongside each other and change the growing culture of hate. We need to make sure our LGBTQ+ communities feel safe and welcome in our community.

I believe local businesses need to be fairly considered for council contracts, rather than prioritising national companies with no interest in ward issues. Decisions made in development need to strive to preserve our nature and be sensitive to biodiversity, rather than only considering commercial growth.

I hope to be a voice for these key issues on the council and listen to resident needs to ensure my opinions are informed by local experience.

Hi, I’m Nick and I’m proud to be standing for the Green Party in Liversedge & Gomersal. I have lived and worked in the area for over two decades. I am currently the Chief Executive of a medium sized advice charity working across Kirklees. As a social welfare lawyer, and as a trustee for a number of charities, I see many of the problems people are facing at the moment – and, very often, I see how caring and dedicated people can provide support and find solutions.

I am keen to work hard to achieve the change we need both locally and nationally to address cost of living issues, improve the services we all need for health, housing, education and transport, and more widely to protect our environment for future generations. Some of this can seem very big picture stuff, but I know from experience that so much can be done by individuals at a local level working together for a better future.

I would like to briefly introduce myself and give you a background of my history. Not for sympathy but to highlight the fact that I have so much first-hand experiences, that it is easier for me to empathise with others and to be able to find help and offer support.

I was raised with a brother with severe disabilities. My mum suffered with mental health issues and consequently I became a young carer, spending lots of time visiting both in hospital while struggling with issues myself. I became a mum at the age of 17 and I’m pleased and proud to say I have two amazing sons who work hard, and I also am pleased to say that I have a beautiful granddaughter now also.

After my brother passed away in 2015 I felt passionate about using my time to help in the community wherever needed. I have been heavily involved with organising events such as the Batley festival, Heckmondlight and the great get together. I have organised summer fairs for residents on my estate and Christmas lunches, helping people in the local community connect with others and make new friends. I have volunteered as an NSPCC school service volunteer delivering workshops and assemblies to both key stage one and key stage two children. sadly, there is an increase in need to remind children that they have the right to say no and that there is always someone to turn to if they feel unsafe.

I am part of the North Kirklees interfaith group and have worked with refugees, sign posting them to different services and helping them become active in the community. I am also a volunteer co-ordinator with the Royal Voluntary Service. Before Covid, I was running intergenerational groups helping senior people and children connect with each other.

Most recently I have opened a community café which provides opportunities for people to gain work experience and build confidence. It also provides a place for support groups to meet regularly, and other activity sessions will be available.

One of the main issues that

Hi, I’m Catherine. I have lived in Mirfield for over a decade and run a small business here. I am Chair of Mirfield Community Trust and spend much of my time helping to run Mirfield Community Centre. I am proud that over the last few years we have developed this beautiful building from the fairly run-down state it was in when it was handed over by the Council into a well loved asset for the community. We have also made it a showcase for becoming a green community building – with significant insulation and solar panels we are close to becoming net zero which benefits the environment but has also saved thousands on our energy bills to help make it financially sustainable.
Mirfield is often the forgotten place between Huddersfield and Dewsbury and, if elected, I would work to raise our profile and bring improvements to the ward. We are likely to have a progressive coalition of Greens and others running the council after May and I would work positively with the new executive for the benefit of the Mirfield area.

Andrew Cooper has been a Green Party Councillor in Huddersfield for over 25 years. He initiated the UK's first universally free insulation scheme and represented the UK on the European Union Committee of the Regions between 2015 and 2020. He has also been a delegate at several UNFCC Climate Change talks.
